name: Face Swap Privacy and Consent Decision Matrix v1
purpose: Separate the privacy, consent, retention, training-use, account-linkage, disclosure and takedown questions that should be checked before uploading or publishing face-swapped media.

scope.jurisdiction: Global decision aid with selected official EU, UK and US references

method.firstPartyObservation: FaceSwapAI product facts, privacy policy, terms and takedown page were reviewed on 2026-07-16.
method.externalGuidance: Selected government and standards sources were used to define decision questions about synthetic-content transparency, biometric-data processing and non-consensual intimate imagery.

statusDefinitions.stated: A current first-party FaceSwapAI source publishes the boundary.
statusDefinitions.provider_dependent: FaceSwapAI publishes a first-party boundary but upstream-provider rules also apply.
statusDefinitions.not_blanket_guaranteed: The reviewed FaceSwapAI sources do not publish a universal guarantee covering every upstream processor.
statusDefinitions.user_responsibility: The user must obtain rights, consent or approval for the intended use.
statusDefinitions.prohibited: The current FaceSwapAI terms prohibit the use.
statusDefinitions.jurisdiction_dependent: The applicable duty depends on location, role, context or another legal fact.

normalizationRules: Task-file retention is recorded separately from account, payment, security, support and legal records.
A deletion statement is not treated as evidence about model-training use.
A first-party FaceSwapAI statement is not extended to an upstream provider unless the source explicitly does so.
Consent to create an edit is not treated as consent to every publication or commercial context.
A product checkbox records the user's confirmation but is not treated as the underlying permission record.
A disclosure label can reduce confusion but is not treated as permission for an otherwise prohibited or unlawful use.

productDecisionMatrix.question: Is generation anonymous?
productDecisionMatrix.answer: No. Generation requires sign-in, a verified email address and consent confirmation.

productDecisionMatrix.question: How long are uploaded and generated task files kept?
productDecisionMatrix.answer: FaceSwapAI schedules uploaded and generated task files for deletion after 24 hours and may remove them earlier. Upstream-provider retention policies also apply.
doesNotCover: copies downloaded, messaged, backed up or published by the user

productDecisionMatrix.question: Does the 24-hour schedule prove that no processor uses task media for model training?
productDecisionMatrix.answer: No. Retention and model-training use are separate disclosures. The reviewed FaceSwapAI sources publish the task-file schedule and upstream-provider boundary but do not establish a blanket training-use guarantee for every upstream processor.
productDecisionMatrix.buyerCheck: Require a current explicit policy or written answer if training use is material to the project.

productDecisionMatrix.question: Can task media be processed by another provider?
productDecisionMatrix.answer: Yes. Task media may be transmitted to an upstream AI provider to complete the requested processing, and provider-side processing and deletion rules also apply.
notStated: a public name for the current proprietary upstream provider
a guarantee that one model architecture is always used

productDecisionMatrix.question: Does task-file deletion erase every record associated with an account?
productDecisionMatrix.answer: No. Account, order, payment, fraud-prevention, security and support records may be retained longer when needed for operation, disputes, abuse prevention or legal duties.

productDecisionMatrix.question: Does a free photo trial reveal the account email?
productDecisionMatrix.answer: Yes. The eligible trial visibly includes FaceSwapAI.com and the complete, unmasked account email. Buying credits does not alter that existing file; the user must generate a new paid result.
productDecisionMatrix.paidBoundary: A newly generated paid result contains neither the photo-trial watermark nor the account email.

productDecisionMatrix.question: Does access to a public photo create permission to alter or publish the person's likeness?
productDecisionMatrix.answer: No. The user must own or be authorized to use the files and must have permission from every depicted person for the intended edit and publication context.

productDecisionMatrix.question: Can the service be used with minors?
productDecisionMatrix.answer: No. FaceSwapAI is intended for users aged 18 or older, and the current policy instructs users not to upload the face of a minor.

productDecisionMatrix.question: Which high-harm uses are prohibited?
productDecisionMatrix.answer: Current terms prohibit non-consensual intimate imagery, sexual material involving minors, harassment, deceptive impersonation, fraud, deceptive political content, unlawful surveillance, rights violations, malicious code, limit circumvention and unlawful purposes.

productDecisionMatrix.question: When should a face swap be disclosed?
productDecisionMatrix.answer: Disclose realistic edits when viewers could reasonably mistake the media for an authentic event, statement, endorsement or participation. Specific duties depend on role, context and jurisdiction.

productDecisionMatrix.question: How can a user request access, correction, deletion or objection?
productDecisionMatrix.answer: Email support@faceswapai.com. FaceSwapAI may verify identity and may retain records needed for legal, payment, security or abuse-prevention reasons.

productDecisionMatrix.question: How can non-consensual intimate imagery be reported?
productDecisionMatrix.answer: Email support@faceswapai.com with the subject 'Urgent NCII removal' and include the exact URL or task, identification, contact information, a good-faith statement that publication lacked consent and a signature.

preUploadChecklist: Identify the owner and license for every source, target, audio and text asset.
Obtain consent from every identifiable person for the intended edit, audience and publication context.
Do not upload a minor's face or use the service for a prohibited high-harm context.
Review task-file retention separately from account-record retention and upstream-provider processing.
Require an explicit model-training statement when training use is material; do not infer it from deletion language.
Assume the eligible free-trial result will visibly expose the complete account email.
Plan a persistent disclosure for realistic media that could be mistaken for authentic.
Keep consent evidence and the escalation contact separate from the publicly shared media.

officialReferences.title: Code of Practice on Transparency of AI-Generated Content
Reducing Risks Posed by Synthetic Content: An Overview of Technical Approaches to Digital Content Transparency
officialReferences.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology

officialReferences.title: Guidance on AI and Data Protection
TAKE IT DOWN Act, Public Law No. 119-12

limitations: This matrix is a dated decision aid, not legal advice.
It is not an independent penetration test, privacy certification, provider audit or model-training audit.
Laws, product behavior, providers and published policies can change after the observation date.
A stated product boundary does not establish that a user's intended publication is lawful or ethically appropriate.
Not blanket guaranteed does not assert that training occurs; it records that the reviewed evidence does not establish a universal exclusion.
